mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 6.5% over the next 3 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 6.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% decline in the housing market index over the next 2 months signals a weakening housing market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST < 90% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in the industrial production index over the next 3 months signals a strengthening supply chain that reduces production costs and increases corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in the real GDP growth rate over the next 2 months signals a strengthening economy that increases consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 2% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% decline in the employment rate over the next 3 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 130000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in the consumer price index over the next 2 months signals a strengthening inflationary environment that reduces purchasing power and increases interest rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 2% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in the real disposable personal income over the next 3 months signals a strengthening economy that increases consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 395000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
